name: Mental Practice — 20 min session
description: Score study + visualization + aural rehearsal of one passage. No instrument required. Practice on a plane, in bed, on a walk.

items.label: Pick the passage
items.instruction: Choose one passage you're currently learning. Ideally 8-32 measures. Something you can already play but haven't fully memorized.

items.label: Score study — 8 minutes
items.instruction: Open the score (or your ear-transcribed sketch). Read it silently, hearing each note in your head. Track your fingers on your leg or in the air. When you hit a spot you can't hear internally, stop. Figure out what's happening theoretically (what chord, what mode, what interval) until you can hear it. This is the highest-payoff mental practice mode.

items.label: Visualization — 6 minutes
items.instruction: Close your eyes. Play the passage from start to finish in your head. See your hands moving. Hear every note. When your mind wanders or a section blurs, that's diagnostic — that section isn't as solid as you thought. Note it.

items.label: Aural rehearsal — 4 minutes
items.instruction: Just listen internally. Play the passage in your head without any physical motion. If a phrase is unclear in your head, it will be unclear in performance. Complete audibility is the goal.
